enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: Heat rolls from the forge in waves. The air tastes of iron and charcoal. A broad man stands at the anvil, hammer resting on his shoulder, watching you with the patience of someone who measures time in blade-lengths.

hello.speaker: kael
hello.text: You need something made, or you need something fixed. Either way, bring materials and coin. The anvil does the rest.

craft-info.speaker: kael
craft-info.text: Blades, mail, potions if you bring the petals. Check the anvil — it knows what I can do better than I can explain it.

materials.speaker: kael
materials.text: Iron from the mines past Greenhollow. Pelts and bone from whatever is trying to kill you. Moonpetals grow where the forest thins. Bring them here. I do not go looking for trouble — I shape what trouble leaves behind.

leave.speaker: kael
leave.text: The forge stays hot. Come back when you have something worth hammering.
